children.name: Eurypyga
children.description: A monotypic genus of elegantly patterned wading bird with an extraordinary hidden display. The Sunbittern's subtle brown, grey and black plumage conceals brilliant orange, red and black bands on the wings that are flashed open in a dramatic sunburst display during courtship and threat. It inhabits forest streams and rivers of the Neotropics.

children.name: Eurypyga helias
children.description: A heron-like bird found along forest streams and rivers from Guatemala south through the Amazon basin. When alarmed or courting, it opens its wings to reveal a stunning pattern of rich orange, chestnut, black and white that resembles the rising sun. It feeds by stabbing at fish, frogs, and aquatic invertebrates with its sharp bill.
